expectations.text: The response mentions the Desktop app's built-in browser or Chrome DevTools integration for testing web servers
expectations.evidence: Says 'Claude Code itself does not have a built-in browser'. Describes MCP-based Chrome DevTools approach instead of the Desktop app's native embedded browser/preview feature.

expectations.text: The response explains how Claude can automatically start and interact with a web server
expectations.evidence: Describes starting via Bash tool and interacting via Chrome DevTools MCP (navigate, screenshot, click, fill forms, etc.).

expectations.text: The response mentions how to set up similar functionality in the CLI (e.g. via MCP or chrome-devtools)
expectations.evidence: Provides detailed CLI + MCP workflow with .mcp.json example for chrome-devtools MCP server.
